description: Canonical tool definitions for the lorekeep tool surface. The rusty-crew adapter-lorekeep package generates or derives its tool definitions from this file rather than hand-writing them (no hand-written protocol copies). Every tool here is backed by a lorekeep HTTP endpoint. Session and coordination tools the narrator/mechanic also use (get_scene_state, get_rp_history, get_last_scene_brief, get_system_prompt, get_profile_config, get_provider_patterns, apply_proposal/reject_proposal, and the non-lore propose_* tools) are backed by rusty-crew session state, NOT lorekeep, so they are intentionally out of scope for this contract — the adapter composes them separately. lorekeep knows nothing about rusty-crew internals.

narrator.description: Lorekeep tools available to the narrator agent during Phase 1 (explore → compose). Read tools assemble the scene brief; capture_fact is the only write.

mechanic.description: Lorekeep tools available to the mechanic (OOC diagnostic) agent. Reads inspect lore and retrieval behavior; lore writes are staged as proposals for user approval.

tools.name: search_lore
tools.description: FTS5 search over campaign lore entries. Returns raw matches without scoring, budgeting, or subject filtering. Backed by POST /api/entries/search.

tools.name: recall_lore
tools.description: Scored, budgeted recall packet from lorekeep. Honors active/excluded subjects for bleed prevention. Backed by POST /api/recall; request matches recall-request.schema.json, response matches recall-packet.schema.json.

active_subjects.description: Subject slugs present in the current scene.

excluded_subjects.description: Subject slugs NOT in the scene (zeroed via excluded_subject_penalty).

token_budget.description: Maximum tokens for the recall packet.

tools.name: explore_topic
tools.description: Follow topic-graph edges from a node for multi-hop world exploration. Backed by the topic graph read API.

node_slug.description: Slug of the starting topic node.

tools.name: capture_fact
tools.description: Record an established fact to lorekeep during play. Backed by POST /api/facts/capture; request matches fact-capture.schema.json.

canon_level.description: Authority level; captured facts are usually session_canon.

established_in_session.description: RP session in which the fact was established.

capture_reason.description: Why the fact is being captured.

tools.name: get_recall_logs
tools.description: Retrieval traces for an RP session — what lore was considered, retrieved, and skipped, and why. Backed by GET /api/traces/by-session/{session_id}; entries match retrieval-trace.schema.json.

session_id.description: RP session to fetch traces for.

tools.name: get_retrieval_config
tools.description: Per-campaign retrieval configuration (depth, thresholds, boosts, filters). Backed by GET /api/config/{campaign_id}; response matches retrieval-config.schema.json.

campaign_id.description: Campaign whose retrieval config to read.

tools.name: get_established_facts
tools.description: Facts captured during play for a campaign. Backed by an entries query filtered to entry_kind=established_fact.

campaign_id.description: Campaign whose established facts to list.

tools.name: propose_lore_add
tools.description: Stage a proposal to add a new lore entry. Produces a proposal payload for user approval — it does not write directly. entry_data matches entry.schema.json.

reason.description: Why this entry should be added.

tools.name: propose_lore_edit
tools.description: Stage a proposal to edit a lore entry's body. Produces a proposal payload for user approval.

entry_slug.description: Slug of the entry to edit.

reason.description: Why this edit improves the entry.

tools.name: propose_lore_tag_update
tools.description: Stage a proposal to adjust the tags on a lore entry for better/worse retrieval. Produces a proposal payload for user approval.

entry_slug.description: Slug of the entry to retag.

tools.name: propose_retrieval_config_change
tools.description: Stage a proposal to adjust a campaign's lorekeep retrieval config field (depth, threshold, boosts, etc.). Produces a proposal payload for user approval.

campaign_id.description: Campaign whose retrieval config to change.

new_value.description: Proposed new value for the field.
